elements.title: Point Clear Martello Tower
elements.contents: ... that the 19th-century Point Clear Martello Tower (pictured) now houses an aviation museum?

elements.title: Jimmy Whalen
elements.contents: ... that, when baseball pitcher Jimmy Whalen was promoted to the Major Leagues, he refused to report because the team would not pay his travel expenses?

elements.title: Australian Antarctic Territory
elements.contents: ... that Australia took Japan to the International Court of Justice for its whaling activities near the Australian Antarctic Territory?

elements.title: Christopher Morcom
elements.contents: ... that Alan Turing had a childhood crush on Christopher Morcom?

elements.title: Gropius House
elements.contents: ... that a design competition for a bathroom outside the Gropius House attracted 280 entries?

elements.title: Nyamazana
elements.contents: ... that warrior-queen Nyamazana defeated the last Rozvi ruler, an act that has been miscredited to a male relative or her husband?

elements.title: a recent merger of American television station operators
elements.contents: ... that one opponent of a recent merger of American television station operators called it "the most massive TV consolidation in history using a kangaroo process"?

elements.title: Jōsaku Maeda
elements.contents: ... that Japanese painter Jōsaku Maeda began consciously painting mandalas after critic Konstanty Jeleński described his Night Series as such in Paris in 1959?

elements.title: Bite Me
elements.contents: ... that the choreography of "Bite Me" prompted fans to send competing protest trucks to the record label over the use of female backup dancers?
